---
title: "How to Use This Lab"
subtitle: "The workflow, the confidence grades, and the tasks you will actually do"
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Start Here <span class="sep">›</span> How to Use This Lab
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**This page is the manual.** It explains how to navigate, how to read confidence, and how to run the
three common tasks.
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="pc-next">[Open the Decisions](../decisions/index.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

## How to navigate

The navbar follows the workflow, not the folders. Move left to right as your thinking matures:

::: {.lifecycle}
<li><span class="stage">Decisions</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage">Evidence</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage">Frameworks</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage">Dashboards</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage is-loop">Recommendations</span></li>
:::

Each section carries its own left sidebar, and every major page opens with a **context banner** that
tells you where you are, which decision the page supports, its confidence, and where to go next.

## How to read confidence grades

Every claim and recommendation carries a grade. Do not read a number without reading its grade.

| Grade | Meaning | Typical basis |
|---|---|---|
| <span class="chip chip-grade-d">D</span> | **Hypothesis** — asserted, not evidenced | Proposal claim only |
| <span class="chip chip-grade-c">C</span> | **Indicative** — weak / illustrative | Synthetic data, single source |
| <span class="chip chip-grade-b">B</span> | **Substantiated** — corroborated | Multiple independent sources, validated method |
| <span class="chip chip-grade-a">A</span> | **Validated** — proven and monitored | Convergent evidence + live metrics |

At v0.1, essentially everything is **C or D**. The [Claims & Confidence](../evidence/claims-confidence.qmd)
page shows the live grade for every claim.

## The three tasks you will actually do

::: {.purpose-grid}
::: {.pg-cell}
::: {.pg-label}
1 · Read the state
:::
::: {.pg-val}
Open a [decision](../decisions/index.qmd) or [dashboard](../dashboards/index.qmd) to see the current
illustrative ranking and the reasoning behind it.
:::
:::
::: {.pg-cell}
::: {.pg-label}
2 · Stress-test it
:::
::: {.pg-val}
Edit a framework's weights in its YAML file, re-render, and watch the ranking move. This is how you
challenge "why is this #1?".
:::
:::
::: {.pg-cell}
::: {.pg-label}
3 · Add evidence
:::
::: {.pg-val}
Add a row to the [Evidence Register](../evidence/evidence-register.qmd); the claim's strength and
grade recompute automatically.
:::
:::
:::

## The rule that keeps it honest

A decision cannot become a recommendation until its evidence clears **grade B**. Each
[decision page](../decisions/index.qmd) states exactly what evidence it still needs. Until then, the
decision stays open — by design.

## Where to go next

- [Current Status](current-status.qmd) — the live state of all three decisions.
- [Decisions](../decisions/index.qmd) — start working.
- [Governance › Testing Standard](../governance/testing-standard.qmd) — how the numbers are guarded.
